context: Tests whether the agent correctly identifies and applies the appropriate semantic version bump type when a skill gains new backward-compatible capabilities. The agent must update tile.json and document their reasoning. Graded by inspecting tile.json and process.md.

checklist.name: Semver format preserved
checklist.description: The new version in tile.json follows x.y.z format (three dot-separated integers)

checklist.name: Minor bump applied
checklist.description: The new version is 0.2.0 (minor component incremented, patch reset to 0, major unchanged)

checklist.name: Patch bump not used
checklist.description: The new version is NOT 0.1.1 (patch bump would understate the scope of change)

checklist.name: Major bump not used
checklist.description: The new version is NOT 1.0.0 (major bump would overstate a backward-compatible change)

checklist.name: Bump type explained
checklist.description: process.md states which bump type was chosen (e.g. 'minor bump' or 'minor version')

checklist.name: New capability cited
checklist.description: process.md mentions that the change adds new functionality or capability as the reason for the bump

checklist.name: Backward compatibility noted
checklist.description: process.md notes that the change is backward-compatible (existing workflows are unaffected)

checklist.name: Reasoning present
checklist.description: process.md includes a sentence or more explaining the rationale for the version choice (not just stating the new version)

checklist.name: Both files noted
checklist.description: process.md mentions both SKILL.md and tile.json as files to stage or commit
